light_pressure_read.read: Light and pressure do not strongly push the outlook either way.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.

generated_text.quick_read: Trout fishing on the Toccoa River is fair to good with recent stocking supporting active trout feeding. Current flow is moderate, and weather conditions are stable with light winds and partly cloudy skies.
generated_text.best_window: The best window is day 2 morning due to moderate sky cover, cooler temperatures around 69.5°F, and low rain chances, creating comfortable feeding conditions for trout in runs, seams, and structure.
generated_text.current_conditions: Flow is 230 cfs with a gage height of 1.21 ft. Water temperature and turbidity data are unavailable.
generated_text.weather_setup: Average sky cover is around 30%, pressure is steady at 30.06 inHg with no significant trend, winds are light from the west at 3.5 mph, and rain chances are low with only 0.1 inch expected.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout will position near runs, seams, and structure, favoring comfortable feeding lanes especially when light is high. Use a balanced nymph approach with smaller natural patterns and stocked-trout attractors like egg patterns, squirmy worms, and Duracell nymphs for clean drifts and effective presentation.
